title: Zhenan Bao - Chemical Engineering - Stanford University
excerpts:
Section Title: Zhenan Bao
Content:
K. K. Lee Professor, Senior Fellow at the Precourt Institute for Energy and Professor, by courtesy, of Materials Science and Engineering, of Chemistry, and of Bioengineering
Zhenan Bao joined Stanford University in 2004. She is currently a K.K. Lee Professor in Chemical Engineering, and with courtesy appointments in Chemistry, Bioengineering and Material Science and Engineering. She was the Department Chair of Chemical Engineering from 2018-2022 and in 2025. She founded the Stanford Wearable Electronics Initiative (eWEAR) and is the current faculty director. Bao received her Ph.D. degree in Chemistry from The University of Chicago in 1995 and joined Bell Labs, Lucent Technologies. She became a Distinguished Member of Technical Staff in 2001. Professor Bao currently has more than 800 refereed publications and more than 80 US patents with a Google Scholar H-index 237.

title: Prof. Zhenan Bao - BoutiqueVC - Investing for Life
excerpts:
Section Title: Prof. Zhenan Bao
Content:
Scientific Advisor
Stanford University
Member of National Academy of Engineering
Dr. Bao is a renowned chemical engineer and material scientist. She is the K.K. Lee Professor in Chemical Engineering and the Chair of the Department of Chemical Engineering at Stanford University, as well as the Founding Director of the Stanford Wearable Electronics Initiative (eWEAR). Dr. Bao has published over700 refereed publications and more than 100 US patents and is regarded as one of the most highly cited scientists in the world with over 127,000 citations. She has received numerus awards, including the Royal Society of Chemistry Beilby Medal and the L'Oreal UNESCO Women in Science Award North America Laureate, as well as was elected to the National Academy of Engineering, the American Academy of Arts and Sciences and the National Academy of Inventors.


BIOGRAPHY
Zhenan Bao is K.K. Lee Professor of Chemical Engineering, and by courtesy, a Professor of Chemistry and a Professor of Material Science and Engineering at Stanford University. Bao founded the Stanford Wearable Electronics Initiate (eWEAR) in 2016 and serves as the faculty director.

title: Materials scientist Zhenan Bao is focused on the future
excerpts:
Section Title: Materials scientist Zhenan Bao is focused on the future
Content:
October 18, 2024
Cell Press communications team
Image credit: Przemyslaw Grygiel
Zhenan Bao, PhD, a K.K. Lee Professor of Chemical Engineering at Stanford University, was selected as one of the Cell Press 50 Scientists that Inspire for her game-changing work developing skin-inspired polymer electronics, organic field-effect transistors, and organic semiconductors.
Currently, Dr. Bao’s research aims to develop flexible and stretchable materials that are inspired by human skin. Her group integrates principles from chemistry, physics, and material sciences, and she says she is inspired by “a curiosity to know why things work the way they do and a desire to create something new.”

title: Zhenan Bao
excerpts: **Zhenan Bao** ( Chinese : 鲍哲南 ; pinyin : *Bào Zhé-nán* ; born 1970) is a Chinese-born American chemical engineer. She serves as K. K. Lee Professor of Chemical Engineering at Stanford University , with courtesy appointments in Chemistry and Material Science and Engineering. <sup>[[ 1 ]](:0-1)</sup> She served as the Department Chair of Chemical Engineering from 2018 to 2022. <sup>[[ 1 ]](:0-1)</sup> She was an Associate Editor for the Royal Society of Chemistry journal Chemical Science, Polymer Reviews and Synthetic Metals. Bao is known for her work on organic field-effect transistors and organic semiconductors , for applications including flexible electronics and electronic skin .

title: Professor Zhenan Bao
excerpts:
Skip to content
VinFuture Prize Laureates
Professor Zhenan Bao
Women Innovators | 2021
BIOGRAPHY
Affiliation at the time of the award: Stanford University, Chemical Engineering, Shriram Center
Professor Bao received her Ph.D. degree in Chemistry from The University of Chicago in 1995, and subsequently joined the Materials Research Department of Bell Labs, Lucent Technologies where she became a Distinguished Member of Technical Staff in 2001. Professor Bao then joined Stanford University in 2004 and is currently a K.K. Lee Professor in Chemical Engineering, with courtesy appointments in the Department of Chemistry and the Department of Material Science and Engineering. Professor Bao has been the Department Chair of Chemical Engineering since 2018 and she is the Faculty Director of the Stanford Wearable Electronics Initiative (eWEAR). She is also an affiliated faculty member of the Precourt Institute, Woods Institute, ChEM-H, Bio-X, and an investigator of the Chan-Zuckerberg BioHub.
